timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: Cyrus conquers Babylon
description: The conquest of Babylon and the policies later associated with the Cyrus Cylinder became an enduring reference point in later narratives about rights and religious tolerance.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: Magna Carta is sealed
description: King John of England accepted Magna Carta, a foundational charter that limited royal power and shaped later constitutional and rights traditions.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: Petition of Right is presented
description: The English Parliament asserted protections against arbitrary taxation, imprisonment, and martial law, strengthening the constitutional basis of individual liberties.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: Habeas Corpus Act is enacted
description: The English Habeas Corpus Act reinforced legal safeguards against unlawful detention and became a landmark in the protection of personal liberty.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: English Bill of Rights is adopted
description: The settlement after the Glorious Revolution formally limited the crown and affirmed parliamentary rights and protections against cruel punishments.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: United States Declaration of Independence is issued
description: The declaration proclaimed that all men are created equal and possess unalienable rights, giving enduring political force to natural-rights language.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen is proclaimed
description: The French Revolution produced a major rights charter affirming liberty, equality before the law, sovereignty of the nation, and freedom of expression.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: United States Bill of Rights takes effect
description: The first ten amendments to the U.S. Constitution protected speech, religion, assembly, due process, and other civil liberties.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: British Parliament abolishes the slave trade
description: The Abolition of the Slave Trade Act marked a major rights milestone by outlawing the British transatlantic slave trade.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: First Geneva Convention is signed
description: States adopted rules for the care of wounded soldiers, helping establish the modern humanitarian law tradition.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: Thirteenth Amendment abolishes slavery in the United States
description: The amendment ended slavery and involuntary servitude in the United States except as punishment for crime.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: First Hague Peace Conference convenes
description: The conference produced conventions on the laws and customs of war and advanced the codification of international humanitarian norms.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: International Labour Organization is founded
description: The ILO linked social justice and labor protections to international peace and helped embed economic and social rights in global governance.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: United Nations Charter is signed
description: The charter placed human rights and fundamental freedoms at the center of the postwar international order.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: Universal Declaration of Human Rights is adopted
description: The UN General Assembly adopted the UDHR, the foundational statement of universal human rights in the modern era.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: European Convention on Human Rights is signed
description: European states created a regional human rights system with binding obligations and judicial enforcement.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: Convention on the Elimination of Racial Discrimination is adopted
description: The United Nations adopted a treaty obligating states to combat racial discrimination and segregation.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: Two International Covenants are adopted
description: The ICCPR and the ICESCR transformed the principles of the UDHR into treaty-based legal commitments.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: CEDAW is adopted
description: The Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination against Women became a central treaty for women's rights.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: Convention on the Rights of the Child is adopted
description: The CRC established the most widely ratified global framework for protecting the rights of children.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Human Rights
timeline_description: Major milestones in the development of human rights ideas, declarations, laws, and institutions from antiquity to the modern international system.
title: Rome Statute creates the International Criminal Court
description: The Rome Statute established a permanent court to prosecute genocide, crimes against humanity, and war crimes.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Code of Hammurabi codifies slave status
description: The Babylonian law code included provisions regulating slavery, providing one of the earliest detailed written records of slave status and punishment.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Solon curbs debt slavery at Athens
description: The reforms of Solon ended debt bondage for Athenian citizens, though slavery itself remained embedded in Greek society.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Islamic slave trades expand across Africa and Asia
description: From the early Islamic period, major trans-Saharan and Indian Ocean slave trades connected African, Middle Eastern, and Asian markets.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Portuguese organize a major slave sale at Lagos
description: A public sale of captive Africans at Lagos symbolized the growing Portuguese role in the Atlantic slave trade.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Direct transatlantic slave shipments to the Americas are authorized
description: The Spanish crown authorized direct transport of enslaved Africans to the Americas, accelerating the Atlantic plantation system.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Enslaved Africans arrive in English Virginia
description: The recorded arrival of Africans in Virginia became a pivotal reference point in the history of slavery in English North America.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Somerset judgment limits slavery in England
description: Lord Mansfield's ruling in Somerset v. Stewart held that slavery lacked support in English common law, energizing abolitionist arguments.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Haitian Revolution begins
description: An uprising by enslaved people in Saint-Domingue launched the Haitian Revolution, the most successful slave revolt in modern history.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: France abolishes slavery in its colonies
description: The French National Convention abolished slavery throughout the French colonial empire during the Revolution.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Britain bans the transatlantic slave trade
description: The Abolition of the Slave Trade Act made the British slave trade illegal and marked a turning point in Atlantic abolitionism.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: United States bans the importation of enslaved people
description: A federal ban on the international slave trade took effect in the United States, though domestic slavery continued.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Slavery Abolition Act passes in the British Empire
description: The British Parliament legislated emancipation across most of the empire, with apprenticeship provisions during the transition.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: France abolishes slavery permanently
description: The French Second Republic abolished slavery in French colonies, ending the institution after Napoleon had earlier restored it.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Russia emancipates the serfs
description: The emancipation of millions of serfs did not end all coercive labor, but it marked one of the nineteenth century's largest acts of legal liberation.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Emancipation Proclamation takes effect
description: Abraham Lincoln's proclamation declared freedom for enslaved people in Confederate-held territory and transformed the Civil War.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Thirteenth Amendment abolishes slavery in the United States
description: The amendment constitutionally abolished slavery across the United States.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Brazil abolishes slavery
description: The Lei Áurea, or Golden Law, ended slavery in Brazil, the last country in the Americas to do so.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: League of Nations adopts the Slavery Convention
description: The convention committed states to prevent and suppress the slave trade and work toward abolition of slavery in all its forms.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Universal Declaration of Human Rights prohibits slavery
description: Article 4 of the UDHR declared that no one shall be held in slavery or servitude.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Supplementary Convention targets slavery-like practices
description: The United Nations expanded international law against debt bondage, serfdom, servile marriage, and child exploitation.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Mauritania abolishes slavery by decree
description: Mauritania formally abolished slavery, though enforcement remained weak and antislavery activism continued.

timeline_title: Timeline of Slavery and Abolition
timeline_description: Key developments in the history of slavery, the slave trade, resistance, abolition, and international anti-slavery law.
title: Mauritania criminalizes slavery
description: Mauritania adopted a law making slavery a criminal offense, an important legal step against a long-persisting institution.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Bantu expansions reshape sub-Saharan Africa
description: Long-running Bantu migrations spread languages, agriculture, and ironworking across much of central, eastern, and southern Africa.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Greek colonization expands across the Mediterranean
description: Greek settlers founded new communities from the Black Sea to southern Italy, creating one of the ancient world's major migration systems.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Hunnic movements help trigger the Migration Period
description: The westward advance of the Huns contributed to the large-scale migrations and invasions that transformed late Roman Europe.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Hijra relocates the first Muslim community to Medina
description: The migration of Muhammad and his followers from Mecca to Medina became a foundational event in Islamic history.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Alhambra Decree expels Jews from Spain
description: Spain ordered the expulsion of Jews who refused conversion, creating a major forced migration across the Mediterranean and beyond.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Columbian contact begins Atlantic migration era
description: The voyages of Columbus opened an era of transatlantic settlement, forced labor migration, and demographic transformation.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Mayflower settlers arrive in New England
description: The voyage of the Pilgrims became one of the best-known early migration episodes in English North American colonization.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Great Irish Famine migration begins
description: The famine triggered mass emigration from Ireland, especially to Britain, the United States, Canada, and Australia.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: California Gold Rush draws global migration
description: The gold rush brought migrants from across the United States, Latin America, Europe, and China to California.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Chinese Exclusion Act restricts immigration to the United States
description: The act barred most Chinese labor immigration and became a landmark in exclusionary migration policy.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Armenians are deported from the Ottoman Empire
description: Mass deportations during the Armenian genocide created one of the twentieth century's major forced migrations.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Great Migration begins in the United States
description: African Americans began moving in large numbers from the rural South to northern and western cities.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: United States Immigration Act establishes quota system
description: The Johnson-Reed Act sharply restricted immigration and reshaped migration patterns for decades.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Partition of India displaces millions
description: The partition of British India into India and Pakistan caused one of the largest forced migrations in modern history.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Arab-Israeli war creates a Palestinian refugee crisis
description: War in 1948 displaced hundreds of thousands of Palestinians and created a long-lasting regional refugee question.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Refugee Convention is adopted
description: The UN Convention relating to the Status of Refugees established the core legal framework for refugee protection.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: United States reforms immigration law
description: The Immigration and Nationality Act of 1965 ended the old national-origins quota system and transformed U.S. immigration flows.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Indochinese refugee movements accelerate
description: The end of the Vietnam War and regional conflicts drove large refugee movements from Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Schengen Agreement is signed
description: European states signed the Schengen Agreement, laying groundwork for passport-free movement across much of Europe.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: Syrian refugee crisis begins
description: Civil war in Syria triggered one of the largest refugee crises of the twenty-first century.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Migration
timeline_description: Major episodes of voluntary and forced migration, migration policy, and refugee protection across world history.
title: UN adopts the Global Compact for Migration
description: Member states adopted a nonbinding framework for international cooperation on safe, orderly, and regular migration.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Scribal schools emerge in Sumer
description: In ancient Mesopotamia, formal training for scribes created some of the earliest known organized educational institutions.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Plato founds the Academy
description: Plato's Academy in Athens became one of the most influential institutions in the history of higher learning.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Aristotle founds the Lyceum
description: Aristotle established the Lyceum, advancing systematic inquiry and teaching in philosophy, science, and politics.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Imperial academy is established in Han China
description: The Taixue became a major institution for educating officials in classical learning within imperial China.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: University of Constantinople is founded
description: The imperial school at Constantinople became a major center of advanced learning in the late Roman and Byzantine world.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Al-Qarawiyyin is founded
description: The mosque and educational center at Fez developed into one of the world's oldest continually operating institutions of higher learning.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: University of Bologna takes shape
description: Bologna emerged as a formative model for the medieval European university, especially in the study of law.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Teaching community at Oxford expands
description: Oxford developed into one of Europe's leading universities during the high Middle Ages.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: University of Paris receives formal recognition
description: The University of Paris became a central institution in medieval theology and scholastic learning.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Printing press accelerates educational change
description: Movable-type printing transformed the production of textbooks and the spread of literacy and learning.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Boston Latin School is founded
description: Boston Latin School became the first public school in what became the United States.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Rousseau publishes Émile
description: Jean-Jacques Rousseau's educational philosophy helped reshape modern ideas about childhood and learning.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Horace Mann begins common-school reforms
description: As secretary of the Massachusetts Board of Education, Mann championed tax-supported public schooling and teacher training.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Massachusetts adopts compulsory schooling law
description: Massachusetts enacted the first statewide compulsory school attendance law in the United States.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: United States Department of Education is created
description: The federal government established a Department of Education to collect information on schools and teaching.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Dewey Laboratory School opens
description: John Dewey's school at the University of Chicago became a laboratory for progressive education.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Right to education is affirmed in the UDHR
description: Article 26 of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights declared education a universal right.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: UNESCO adopts convention against discrimination in education
description: The convention targeted exclusion and unequal treatment in education systems.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Title IX is enacted in the United States
description: Title IX prohibited sex discrimination in federally funded education, transforming access and athletics.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: World Conference on Education for All meets at Jomtien
description: Governments and agencies launched a global effort to expand basic education and reduce illiteracy.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Education
timeline_description: Major milestones in the institutional, intellectual, and legal history of education from ancient schools to global mass education.
title: Sustainable Development Goal 4 is adopted
description: The United Nations adopted SDG 4, committing states to inclusive and equitable quality education for all.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Uruk emerges as an early large city
description: Uruk in Mesopotamia became one of the world's first major urban centers, associated with state formation and complex administration.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Indus cities display advanced urban planning
description: Cities such as Mohenjo-daro and Harappa developed planned streets, drainage systems, and standardized building practices.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Babylon rises as a major urban center
description: Babylon became one of the great cities of the ancient Near East, linking political authority, trade, and monumental architecture.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Traditional founding date of Rome
description: Roman tradition dated the founding of Rome to 753 BCE, a city that would become the center of a vast empire.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Alexander founds Alexandria
description: Alexandria became one of the Mediterranean world's leading cities of trade, learning, and empire.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Constantinople is inaugurated
description: Constantine refounded Byzantium as Constantinople, creating a new imperial capital at the crossroads of Europe and Asia.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Baghdad is founded
description: The Abbasid caliphate founded Baghdad, which became one of the world's most important urban centers of learning and commerce.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Heian-kyō is established as Japan's capital
description: The founding of Heian-kyō, later Kyoto, shaped the political and cultural geography of classical Japan.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Kaifeng ranks among the world's largest cities
description: By the Song era, Kaifeng represented the scale and sophistication of premodern Chinese urban life.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Tenochtitlan is founded
description: The Mexica established Tenochtitlan, which became one of the largest and most impressive cities in the pre-Columbian Americas.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Great Fire of London prompts urban rebuilding
description: The destruction caused by the Great Fire led to major rebuilding and long-term changes in urban design and regulation.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Residence Act authorizes Washington as a planned capital
description: The United States chose a new federal capital, creating one of the modern era's most significant planned cities.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Haussmann's renovation of Paris begins
description: Napoleon III empowered Baron Haussmann to redesign Paris with boulevards, infrastructure, and new urban vistas.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Cerdà plan reshapes Barcelona's expansion
description: The Eixample plan offered a highly influential model of modern urban extension and street-grid design.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: Greater New York is consolidated
description: The merger of New York City with surrounding boroughs created the modern metropolis of Greater New York.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
timeline_description: Major milestones in urban history, from early cities and imperial capitals to modern planning and global urbanization.
title: New York adopts landmark zoning resolution
description: The first comprehensive zoning ordinance in the United States helped define modern urban land-use regulation.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Cities
